title: Sensory Neurobiology: A New Crossroads Deep in the Cortex.
abstract: Self-generated movements elicit concomitant sensory responses that are beneficial to ignore. Combining state-of-the-art physiology, behavior, and anatomy, a new study discovers a neural crossroads in the deepest layer of auditory cortex where pre-lick ramping activity suppresses peri-lick sound responses across layers.
